data.content: The Azure page requires auth, so I can’t read its logs directly; I’ll quickly check for CI fragility points (Node/version constraints and lockfile/script expectations) and then give you the most likely failure causes plus exact checks.
I’ve narrowed likely culprits to test snapshots or environment/version mismatch, and I’m now checking terminal history context in this workspace in case the same failure was already run locally.
I found repo guidance that directly matches common CI failures here; I’ll do one last check for Node engine requirements in dependencies so we can rule in/out a pipeline Node-version mismatch.

I found a concrete high-probability issue: your lockfile includes a dependency that requires Node 20.19+, which can break CI immediately if the agent is older; I’m validating that at the top of the lockfile too.

Yes. I cannot open that Azure log directly because it is auth-gated, but I found a very likely root cause from your repo.

Most likely failure
1. CI Node version is too old for current dependencies.
- [package-lock.json](package-lock.json) includes Angular 21 and SKYUX branding builder versions that require newer Node.
- In particular, the lockfile entry for @blackbaud/skyux-branding-builder requires Node >= 20.19.0 at [package-lock.json](package-lock.json#L169).
- If your agent is on Node 18 (or older 20.x), npm ci commonly fails with EBADENGINE / notsup before tests even run.

Second likely failure
1. Snapshot tests are out of date after token changes.
- This repo has snapshot tests in [plugins/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts](plugins/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts).
- Snapshots live in [plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap](plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap).
- The repo README explicitly calls out updating snapshots when token output changes: [README.md](README.md).

What to check in the failed job quickly
1. Search the log for: EBADENGINE, notsup, Unsupported engine, Node version.
2. If found, update pipeline Node to 20.19+ (or 22.12+).
3. Re-run with:
- npm ci
- npm run lint
- npm test
- npm run build

If the failure is snapshot-related
1. Run locally: vitest run --update
2. Commit updated snapshot file:
- [plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap](plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap)

If you paste the first error block from the Azure logs (about 30-50 lines around the first red error), I can pinpoint the exact fix immediately.

is this what you want:  Snapshots  1 failed
             1 obsolete
             ↳ plugins/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts
               · build styles > should generate the correct base, blackbaud, and modern styles 3

 Test Files  2 failed | 1 passed (3)
      Tests  1 failed | 2 passed (3)
   Start at  14:16:29
   Duration  5.68s (transform 139ms, setup 0ms, import 304ms, tests 5.23s, environment 0ms)


##[error]PowerShell exited with code '1'.
Finishing: npm test

Yes, that is exactly the failure signal I needed.

This is a snapshot maintenance failure, not a runtime code crash.  
The key line is:

- 1 obsolete snapshot in [plugins/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts](plugins/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts)

That means your token changes altered generated output, and Vitest now sees one old snapshot entry that no longer matches active assertions (the obsolete “...modern styles 3” case).

Fix
1. Regenerate snapshots locally.
```bash
npm test -- --update
```
2. Confirm the snapshot file changed:
- [plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap](plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap)
3. Review the diff for expected token-output changes, then commit it with your token updates.
4. Push again; CI should pass this stage.

Why this happened
- This repo’s style-dictionary test is snapshot-based in [plugins/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts](plugins/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts).
- When token output changes, snapshots must be updated (also documented in [README.md](README.md)).

If you want, I can also help you sanity-check what changed inside the snapshot diff before you commit.

I reproduced the failure end-to-end and found one more blocker: besides the obsolete snapshot, Vitest is also trying to run an Angular Jasmine spec under [skyux-box-preview/src/app/app.component.spec.ts](skyux-box-preview/src/app/app.component.spec.ts), which causes `describe is not defined`. I’m going to exclude that folder from root Vitest so `npm test` matches intended token tests.
I’ve confirmed the snapshot was updated; now I’m making a minimal config change so root `npm test` only runs this repo’s token specs and stops pulling in the Angular preview app’s Jasmine spec.

data.content:
Fixed. I updated the repo so your CI snapshot failure is resolved and root tests now pass.

What I changed
1. Updated the obsolete snapshot by running Vitest with snapshot update.
- Changed file: [plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap](plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap)

2. Fixed root test discovery so Vitest does not try to run the Angular Jasmine spec from the preview app.
- Updated [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts#L47) to include only token/plugin specs and exclude the preview app folder.

Verification
1. Root test run now passes:
- 2 test files passed
- 3 tests passed
- No failed or obsolete snapshots remaining

Notes
1. I also checked lint, but this workspace has many pre-existing lint errors in generated Angular cache/dist files under skyux-box-preview, which are unrelated to this fix.

What you should do next
1. Commit these two files:
- [plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap](plugins/__snapshots__/build-style-dictionary-plugin.spec.mts.snap)
- [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts)

2. Push and re-run the same Azure build.  
This should clear the npm test failure you posted.

toolRequests.arguments: {"command":"node -e \"const fs=require('fs'); const path=require('path'); const sessionId='4ee4ae93-0b4f-4097-8c6f-f9d627c4ab17'; const source='/Users/pendleton.rouse/Library/Application Support/Code/User/workspaceStorage/78562ccdb4ff378be8a63a7eae5964c7/GitHub.copilot-chat/transcripts/'+sessionId+'.jsonl'; const target=path.resolve('/Users/pendleton.rouse/Documents/GitHub/skyux-design-tokens', 'chat-transcript-backup-'+sessionId+'-2026-05-29.json'); const raw=fs.readFileSync(source,'utf8').split('\\n').filter(Boolean); const entries=raw.map((line, index)=>{ try { return JSON.parse(line); } catch (error) { throw new Error('Failed to parse line '+(index+1)+': '+error.message); } }); const output={ sessionId, source, exportedAt:new Date().toISOString(), entryCount:entries.length, format:'jsonl->json-array', entries }; fs.writeFileSync(target, JSON.stringify(output, null, 2)+'\\n'); console.log(target); console.log('entries='+entries.length);\"","explanation":"Export the current Copilot chat transcript JSONL into a JSON backup file in the repo root","goal":"Create a JSON backup of this conversation","mode":"sync","timeout":600000}
